mapping.a_element: ADE root system associated to the finite subgroup G of SU(2)
mapping.b_element: positive roots of the A_{n-1} gauge group

mapping.a_element: quantum cohomology ring of the minimal resolution Y of C^2/G
mapping.b_element: quantum geometry of the resolution of C^2/Z_n exhibiting roots

mapping.a_element: Frobenius manifold structure on the affine root lattice
mapping.b_element: moduli space of the local geometry with coincident discriminant locus branches

mapping.a_element: Crepant Resolution Conjecture linking quantum cohomology to orbifold Gromov-Witten potential
mapping.b_element: local mirror symmetry relating D-brane central charges to gauge group roots

mapping.a_element: exceptional curves in the classical geometry of the resolution
mapping.b_element: simple roots manifesting as exceptional curves

brief_justification: Both papers use the ADE root system as the core combinatorial/mathematical machinery: paper A constructs a Frobenius manifold on the root lattice via quantum cohomology, while paper B derives the full root system of a non-perturbative gauge group from the quantum geometry of a resolution, with local mirror symmetry playing the role of the Crepant Resolution Conjecture in mapping between geometries.
